expectations.evidence: Lines 3-22 contain a section titled '## 1. 面包屑地图' with a complete ASCII tree showing 4 top-level branches (核心机制、复杂度分析、与归并排序的对比、优化策略) and their子节点, clearly presenting the hierarchical knowledge structure.

Lines 28-44 contain a card labeled '### 全景卡'. Q asks '快速排序作为一种经典排序算法，它的整体知识结构由哪几大块组成？' and A presents a simplified ASCII tree diagram with top-level nodes: 核心机制、复杂度、工程优化, plus a sub-branch for 对比：归并排序.

At least 5 cognitive angles covered: (1) what — e.g. '分治具体分为哪三步' (L50), '不稳定具体指什么' (L125); (2) how — e.g. 流程填空卡 (L62), 三数取中法怎么做 (L133), 尾递归如何减少栈深度 (L145); (3) why — e.g. '为什么时间复杂度是 O(n log n)' (L70), '为什么小数组要切换到插入排序' (L139); (4) reverse — 3 cards under '### Reverse 角度' (L88-104), from result/phenomenon back to cause; (5) 场景/应用 — '为什么标准库选择快速排序' (L155).

Explicit structural separation: '## 2. 核心卡片' (Line 26) contains the main cards organized by subtopic, and '## 3. 补充卡片' (Line 151) contains 2 additional cards. The split is visually and semantically clear.

All answers include sufficient context to be understood without the source material. For example, the partition answer (L58) explains both what happens and what it means; the O(n²) answer (L78) explains the condition, the typical scenario, and the mechanism; the reverse card answer (L92) names the algorithm, explains why, and gives the math. No answer relies on implicit references like '如上所述' or '见前文'.

No answer is a pure bullet-list or numbered-list enumeration. The closest cases: (1) L52 uses inline numbering '(1)...(2)...(3)...' embedded in a prose sentence, which is structured prose rather than a standalone list; (2) L114-121 uses an ASCII comparison table (not a bullet list); (3) L34-43 uses an ASCII tree diagram. All other answers are continuous prose with explanatory reasoning.

Three explicit reverse cards under '### Reverse 角度' (Lines 88-104): (1) From phenomenon (O(n²) on sorted input) to algorithm identification (L90-92); (2) From desired outcome (avoid O(n²)) to root cause (pivot selection strategy) (L96-98); (3) From requirement (stable sort) to why quicksort fails and what to use instead (L102-104).

17 out of 17 cards (100%) have questions starting with a context-positioning phrase. All use patterns like '在快速排序的...中，' or '在排序算法的...中，'. Specific count: '在快速排序...' (12 cards), '在排序算法...' (4 cards), '快速排序作为...' (1 card, panorama). Ratio: 17/17 = 100%, well above the 80% threshold.

The panorama card answer (Lines 34-43) shows exactly 3 top-level module nodes: 核心机制、复杂度、工程优化, with a 4th sub-branch '对比：归并排序'. This is within the 3-5 top-level modules range. It is clearly simplified compared to the full breadcrumb tree (which has 4 branches and ~12 leaf nodes). The panorama card uses compact parenthetical annotations rather than enumerating all sub-items.
